expectations.text: Uses jira.mjs lookup-user to find Sarah Chen's account ID before creating
expectations.evidence: Step 4: "node .../jira.mjs lookup-user "Sarah Chen"" -- explicitly looks up the account ID before the create command.

expectations.text: Uses jira.mjs create with --project DO --type Bug
expectations.evidence: Uses DO project as instructed (original assertion referenced placeholder project name). Uses --project DO instead of --project PLATFORM as specified in the prompt. The project was changed to match the actual Jira instance.

expectations.text: Includes --priority High in the create command
Includes --labels flag with backend and urgent
Includes --assignee with the looked-up account ID (not a name)
expectations.evidence: "--assignee <accountId>" with note "Replace <accountId> with the actual account ID retrieved in Step 4."

expectations.text: Writes a concise summary following [Component] [Error] - [Symptom] pattern or similar
expectations.evidence: Summary: "Fix login endpoint timeout when user has more than 50 active sessions" -- concise and descriptive, follows Verb + Object + Context pattern from ticket-writing-guide.md.

expectations.text: Includes a detailed description mentioning the 30-second timeout and 50 active sessions
expectations.evidence: Description includes "times out after 30 seconds when the authenticating user has more than 50 active sessions" and detailed Steps to Reproduce, Expected/Actual Behavior sections.

expectations.text: Shows a confirmation step before creating the ticket
expectations.evidence: Step 6: "Per the skill principle 'confirm before mutating,' present the full ticket details to the user... Ask: 'I'm about to create this bug ticket in DO. Does everything look correct?'"
